mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, compressing corporate margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold being breached.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion combined with constrained industrial production signals supply-demand imbalances that drive inflationary pressures, leading to negative equity returns as rate hike expectations materialize.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with a t-statistic magnitude greater than 1.96 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting real GDP growth on equity valuations, producing strongly negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns in tightening regimes do not show a statistically significant negative skew compared to easing regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding corporate earnings fundamentals, which mechanically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window despite positive GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index drops below 95.0, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >50% of instances where INDPRO breaches the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coinciding with elevated inflation forces aggressive Federal Reserve policy normalization, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in forward returns in a multivariate regression over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing starts growth, consumer wealth effects boost discretionary spending, which positively drives S&P 500 forward returns only when real interest rates remain below 2.0%.
failure_condition: Positive housing starts fail to correlate with positive SPX returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% annually.
